title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: Arthur Dent is swept off Earth just before it's destroyed, embarking on a wild intergalactic adventure with his alien friend Ford Prefect. Along the way they encounter absurd bureaucracies, a depressed robot, and the answer to life itself.

title: War and Peace
description: Tolstoy's epic novel follows several aristocratic families against the backdrop of the Napoleonic invasion of Russia. It weaves together personal struggles, political intrigue, and philosophical meditation on history and free will.

In a totalitarian future state, Winston Smith struggles to maintain his individuality under the constant surveillance of Big Brother. A chilling exploration of censorship, propaganda, and the corruption of truth.

title: Pride and Prejudice
description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates issues of morality, reputation, and marriage in Regency-era England. Her evolving relationship with the proud Mr. Darcy forms the heart of Austen's witty social commentary.

title: Dune
description: Paul Atreides navigates political betrayal and ecological transformation on the desert planet Arrakis. His rise from exiled duke to messianic leader reshapes the entire galactic order.

title: The Great Gatsby
description: Nick Carraway becomes entangled in the opulent and tragic world of millionaire Jay Gatsby, whose obsessive love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan exposes the hollowness of the American Dream.

title: Brave New World
description: In a future where humans are genetically engineered and pacified by pleasure, John the Savage struggles to find meaning in a world without suffering. Huxley's dystopia questions the cost of a perfectly ordered society.

title: The Lord of the Rings
description: Frodo Baggins and a fellowship of diverse companions embark on a perilous quest to destroy a dark ring of power. Tolkien's masterwork blends rich mythology with themes of courage, friendship, and the corrupting nature of power.

title: Catch-22
description: Captain Yossarian tries to survive the absurdity of war while trapped in a military bureaucracy where only the insane are fit to fly, but asking to be grounded proves you're sane enough to keep flying.

title: Crime and Punishment
description: Raskolnikov, a destitute student in St. Petersburg, commits murder and then wrestles with guilt, morality, and redemption. Dostoevsky's psychological masterpiece probes the depths of human conscience.

title: The Name of the Wind
description: Kvothe recounts his transformation from a talented orphan to the most notorious wizard of his age. Rothfuss crafts a lush, lyrical tale of music, magic, and the dangerous pursuit of knowledge.

title: Good Omens
description: An angel and a demon team up to prevent the apocalypse, having grown rather fond of the world they were supposed to end. Gaiman and Pratchett deliver a wildly irreverent comedy about the nature of good and evil.

title: Frankenstein
description: Victor Frankenstein's obsessive creation of life from dead matter unleashes a creature whose loneliness and rage lead to tragedy. Shelley's gothic novel examines the perils of unchecked ambition and the nature of humanity.

title: The Martian
description: Stranded on Mars after a dust storm forces his crew to evacuate, astronaut Mark Watney must use his ingenuity and dark humor to survive on a hostile planet with limited supplies.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
description: The multi-generational saga of the Buendia family in the fictional Colombian town of Macondo. Marquez blends magical realism with political allegory to create a luminous meditation on time, memory, and the inescapable patterns of history.
